data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding attributable to this child, and NO uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" → 33d0 AC1 (exactly one outbound applier path remains in production). FULLY.
- Parent AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" → 33d0 AC1 (retired path deleted; no dead, separately-tested applier code). FULLY.
- Parent SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 AC1 + AC2 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — driven through the production path, each sub-op asserted to take effect). FULLY.
- Contributes to parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC3: no capability regresses, suite green) and to parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract; zero change-detector" (33d0 AC4: combined test asserts effects/sub-op outcomes, never interaction/mock-call-counts).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "test validates fetcher snapshot vs schema" + AC "test validates each differ's reads vs schema" + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on shape" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake honesty).
- AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" → 8937 AC3 + fe57.
- AC "sub-op telemetry surfaced" + AC "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first)" + SC "silent no-op fails canary" → 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3).
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a.

CORRECTION TO PRIOR AUDITS ON THIS TICKET — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN for this pairing. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads verbatim: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly NAMES its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937's description states it "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), this criterion is FULLY covered, not partial. The prior "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed. (The most recent G3 audit on sibling 2359 already withdrew the same false positive.)

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent "collapse to single production path", parent "retired path deleted", parent SC "production path exercised, not dead path" (all owned by THIS child); CONTRIBUTED — parent AC "suite green" + parent SC "zero change-detector"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip/telemetry/probe criteria (8937/fe57/59ef/2359/822a); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.
